Headline: How the Energy Shock Has Upended the Global Economy
Shock/Event Description: Russia’s invasion disrupted global energy supply and caused major oil and gas shortages.
Quantitative Relationship Extracted: Brent crude oil increased more than 50% during the shock period.
Market Impact: S&P 500 entered bear market territory; volatility increased sharply.
Transmission Mechanism: Oil ↑ → Inflation ↑ → Fed tightening ↑ → Equity valuations ↓

Headline: History Suggests Oil Shock Raises Probability of U.S. Recession
Shock/Event Description: Rapid rise in oil prices increased fears of recession and weaker consumer demand.
Quantitative Relationship Extracted: Major oil spikes historically associated with materially higher recession probability.
Market Impact: Risk-off sentiment increased; growth stocks weakened.

Headline: Rising Oil Prices, Loose Monetary Policy, and US Inflation
Shock/Event Description: Oil-price increases amplified already rising inflation pressures in the US economy.
Quantitative Relationship Extracted: US CPI inflation exceeded 9% in mid-2022 during the energy shock period.
Market Impact: Bond yields rose sharply; equity multiples compressed.
Transmission Mechanism: Oil ↑ → CPI ↑ → Rates ↑ → Financial conditions tighten

Headline: How Could Oil Price and Policy Rate Hikes Affect Inflation?
Shock/Event Description: The Fed analyzed combined effects of oil shocks and monetary tightening.
Quantitative Relationship Extracted: Energy shocks significantly increased short-term inflation expectations.

Headline: Russia Energy Chaos Triggers Biggest Market Shock in Decades
Shock/Event Description: Sanctions and supply disruptions created broad commodity-market instability.
Quantitative Relationship Extracted: Volatility indices surged as commodity prices spiked globally.
Transmission Mechanism: Energy disruption ↑ → Market volatility ↑ → Risk appetite ↓

Headline: How Inflationary Is the Oil Price Spike?
Shock/Event Description: JPM analyzed historical effects of oil spikes on inflation and equities.
Quantitative Relationship Extracted: Energy-sector equities outperformed while growth sectors underperformed during the oil shock.
Market Impact: Sector rotation toward energy and commodities.

Headline: What Recent Oil Spikes Could Mean for Stocks
Shock/Event Description: Analysis of sector sensitivity to rising oil prices.
Quantitative Relationship Extracted: Airline and consumer sectors historically underperformed during oil spikes.
Market Impact: Consumer discretionary and airline stocks weakened.

Headline: Crude Oil Prices Increased in First Half of 2022
Shock/Event Description: Official report documenting magnitude of oil-price increases during 2022.
Quantitative Relationship Extracted: WTI crude oil increased approximately 40% in the first half of 2022.
